input.title: The Waiting Room
input.description: A documentary that spent two years in the waiting room of a free legal clinic, following five clients through the immigration system. The film never leaves the clinic — everything is observed from the perspective of the waiting room, where hope, bureaucracy, and human complexity intersect daily.

key_subjects.name: Miriam Adeyemi
key_subjects.role: Client from Nigeria, awaiting asylum decision

input.director_note: I wanted to make a film about waiting — specifically about what waiting does to people when the thing they are waiting for is their entire future.

input.title: First Light
input.description: A short documentary about lighthouse keepers on a remote island.

input.title: Les Murs Parlent
input.description: A documentary following three street artists working in Paris, Berlin, and Athens, exploring how political murals function as journalism in cities where walls carry more urgency than newspapers. Shot over 18 months, the film captures the creation and destruction of political art in real time — walls painted, buffed, repainted, and arrested for.

key_subjects.name: Zara
key_subjects.role: Parisian muralist who works only at night, identity partially concealed

key_subjects.name: Klaus Richter
key_subjects.role: Berlin-based artist whose work addresses the housing crisis

key_subjects.name: Dimitra Papadopoulos
key_subjects.role: Athens graffiti writer whose political murals have been subject to police removal

input.director_note: In Europe, the wall is the oldest newspaper. I wanted to ask who is writing on it now, and why the authorities keep trying to make them stop.
input.festival_history: Cannes Directors' Fortnight 2025 (World Premiere), DOK Leipzig 2025 (International Competition)
critical_quotes: 'A thrilling act of cultural journalism disguised as an art documentary.' — Screen Daily
'Beaumont's finest and most politically urgent work.' — Positif
